title: dysphania geoffreyi
intro: Dysphania geoffreyi, also known as Chenopodium goryoides, is a species of flowering plant in the amaranth family that is native to Western Australia and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Dysphania geoffreyi is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and aiding in digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular remedy in some herbal traditions.
